question: How should fire extinguishers be stored on board and where should they be placed?
content:
Keep at least one full 5-B (B-1) fire extinguisher aboard; two are recommended. Mount one extinguisher in the lockable storage compartment, using a chemical-type extinguisher with a capacity of 2 lb or more.<PIC>
If a second extinguisher is carried, mount it in the battery compartment at the labeled location outside the compartment.<PIC> Because that extinguisher is near the engine compartment, use a clean-agent type that displaces oxygen, such as CO2 or FE-36, with a capacity of 5 lb or more.
ret: "Keep at least one full 5-B (B-1) fire extinguisher aboard; two are recommended. Mount one extinguisher in the lockable storage compartment, using a chemical-type extinguisher with a capacity of 2 lb or more.<PIC>\nIf a second extinguisher is carried, mount it in the battery compartment at the labeled location outside the compartment.<PIC> Because that extinguisher is near the engine compartment, use a clean-agent type that displaces oxygen, such as CO2 or FE-36, with a capacity of 5 lb or more.",["Manual09_211", "Manual09_212"]

evidence_refs.notes: Fire extinguisher check: one 5-B (B-1) type fire extinguisher is required in USCG-controlled waters; at least one must be aboard and full, and two are recommended.
Storage section: one extinguisher mounts in the lockable storage compartment; a second, if used, mounts in the battery compartment at the labeled location, with specified type and capacity for each.
Shows the fire extinguisher placement in the lockable storage compartment.
Shows the fire extinguisher placement in the battery compartment.

notes: Answered from the Yamaha 210FSH 2021 fire extinguisher check and storage section. Two nearby figures were inspected and aligned with the two placement sentences.
